reviews.review_description: Just started the campaign and I really like it but the graphics and textures could be way better, compared to other games looks like it's still not fully optimized for Quest 3. Also a real co-op campaign for 2 Players would be wonderful. But I’m very thankful that it is cross-buy, so I enjoyed the much better graphics on PCVR.
You have to use some kind of very weird movement were you have to use joysticks in your mech and are really slow game seems good but isnt i wanted to refund but couldnt

reviews.review_title: Love the mech gameplay, needs less fetch
reviews.review_description:
5 star mech gameplay, great level of variation in the worlds and missions, mech types and weapons, enemy and friendly AI, in-game writing is all great. Genuinely nails what we imagined playing 90s PC mech games and the potential for VR interfaces.

Unfortunately, and it pains me to say this because it's clear a lot of work went into the non-mech parts, those are a 2-3 star exp in a way that prevents me from recommending this to everyone I know. Even pumping the button to skip dialog every other mission consists of walking around the ship to pick up objects, but only after talking to the right person, between tapping the watch to figure out what it's waiting to have happen next. I honestly feel like I'm completing a chore getting through it before I turn off the quest to reward myself by being able to do a mech part the next time I play. The plot is good, characters as they are in-mission is good, but if there was an option to replace the non-mech parts with a 2-3 sentence summary or brief cutscene I'd rate this 5 stars easily and it'd be my top recommendation to everyone, enough to buy a quest for. Instead, I'm hesitant to even suggest it to other quest owners without a disclaimer that it'll involve pushing through the non-mech stuff so much between getting to play the (excellent and varied) mech missions.

Minor note: I wish there was a clear indication of how long ago it saved, ambiguity of when checkpoints are leaves me often unsure how much I'll need to replay based on when I exit the game.

Updating my review: discovered today what I really wanted, simply mech combat against bots, is hidden behind the multiplayer menu (Multiplayer->Create Host->Offline Practice, the first two steps of which someone who wants bot skirmish might never click?). I'm most of the way through the single player campaign but I hadn't opened the multiplayer menu, since I don't like to play online. There's a ton of options in there and the bots are solid.

reviews.review_title: An awesome unique vr mech experience
reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

Even for a game that came out a few years ago it's pretty fun, honestly anyone complaining and giving bad ratings 1 either suck at the game because believe it or not you actually have to try, and use some skill, or 2 they for some reason expected like fighting mech bots like unrealistic expectations... The mechs in this are like big mining bots/habitats it's not like jumping in a power suit or something, and with the controls not just being the joystick that's why I personally like it, you actually have to control/pilot the machine.

reviews.review_title: Terrible controls. I want a refund
reviews.review_description: I lve mech games so you can trust I really tried to enjoy this game but the controls are the worst. It’s a gamble whether or not you’re gonna be moving forward or backwards.  You put it in drive or reverse and it just keeps going until you move the throttle again and trying to do that in battle with controls that you can’t actually feel is nearly impossible.  You’ll be fighting to control your mech instead of fighting the enemy.

Never cared about unrealistic robot stuff shooter whatever. Then i had the good idea to open the door and try.

Brilliant because there is pilot skill to develop especially for the thrust rocket feature. Too much you are way off your target with no power left waiting for the energy recharge while being punished  away by gravity.  Hard. Merciless hence depth od game, hence amazing re playability hence a masterpice.
Expectations: epic mech battles. Reality: cardboard robots shining little beams at each other.

I’ve played Onward almost every day for the last three years. Mostly because I love realism and teamplay. 

Sometimes I try something new. And almost every time I am dissapointed. But not with Vox. 

It’s a very immersive game. It took me 4 hours to get to know the controls and one co-op game. 

That co-op was insane. Talking on the radio while steering a giant robot. It blew my mind. 
It felt so real and so inmersive. 


Highly recomend for people who love the hardcore gameplay.

reviews.review_title: Worst mech game I've ever played.
reviews.review_description:
This game is awful.

Everything is slow, clunky and brutally difficult to control, and any mech has any sort of get-up-and-go has armor like tissue paper. You can't even strafe without using the godawful jump jets. Movement inputs feel more like suggestions rather than commands.

The weapons have zero impact. Nothing has any firepower, and they all feel pathetically weak, save for whatever the enemy's using, they'll shred you in seconds.

The flying controls are an exercise in frustration, since fuel recharges at a snail's pace (which wouldn't be so bad if you had more than four seconds worth, tops) and moving in the wrong direction for just a second will send you careening uncontrollably into a wall or off of a cliff or something. It's a perfect storm of way too little acceleration and way too much inertia - not a single mech is immune to this, not even the light ones.

Finally, the game looks HORRIBLE on Quest. I understand the hardware is limited, but every texture is muddy and blurry, human characters look like crude clay sculptures, and the lighting is completely flat.

I wanted to like this game. I love mech/vehicle combat games, but this game is just so bad.

Vox Machinae is VR mech combat done in the worst possible way. You want a good VR mech game, that looks great and makes proper use of the physicality inherent to VR as a medium? Buy UNDERDOGS, that game is excellent.
Wonderful game multiple gamemodes weapons, mechs, maps(with weather and time of day settings) and a wonderful community and community discord
I just got the game 20 minutes ago and all I can say is f*skin WOW! I'm gonna sink my teeth into this o e nice and slowly....

Very good game, the interaction with the Ai players is a bit repetitive. 

Addicting game had a hard time putting it down

Developers. !

In multi player would be nice to see opponents name when u have them targeted , so you can target your friends or humans instead of fighting Bots.  

The Bots on multi player are very good and challenging 

Thanks for the game hope to see some updates

Having grown up with the original XBOX and fondly reminiscing on my days playing Mech Assault with my dad, this game makes me nostalgic and starry-eyed simultaneously! 🤩
5/5⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

See my pros & cons for a full breakdown on why I think this game should be up there with the all-time great games for VR. Like Guardians: Frontline, Dungeons Of Eternity, and Beatsaber... Notice how every one of these games stands out as a leader in their respective game types? That's why Vox Machinae deserves to be played! 😁

Pros: 
- Unbelievably good feeling movement/control mechanics for the mechs. (A.K.A "Grinders)
- Customization is simply mind-blowing. From the cosmetics, to the grinders, to the weapons, to the modifications, to ability to create loadouts, you will never ever feel stuck playing what is "META" as you'll always be able to find unique loadouts that always reward higher skill and ingenuity. 
- The ability to play cross-play, co-op, PVP, PVE, and even a full campaign has me baffled why I haven't seen more players in available multiplayer lobbies. 
- Short range, long-range, fast, slow, air flight/hovering, ramming... Each grinder and loadout comes with the ability to choose how you want to engage your enemies. 

Cons: 
- This game has a big library of tutorials, ranging from the basics, to advanced strategies. (Including advanced abilities/features of the Grinders and weapons) 

Side note (not a con) - > Appropriately, if you complete the basic training, the advanced grinder training, and advanced weapon training, you will unlock a sweet robot avatar. You can also get a robot avatar for completing the campaign. 
- The training can be a bit daunting, and the air & ramming components of the tutorials are particularly challenging. It took persistence and multiple attempts to beat the tutorials on each grinder. (Turning fast is also a bit dizzying sometimes)

Desired improvements: More weapons, maps, and I'd love to have customizable colors for grinders!

This game is the best game for mech lovers, I don’t even like mechs but this is an awesome game. Campaign, fun, lore, gives you a rush. Multiplayer, fun, interactive, lots of choices. There were some parts that were hard and made me rage but I think I’m just bad at the game. If I could give this game a higher ranting, I would. It’s a shame it’s not getting the attention it deserves.

There are many VR games use systems like helmet mounted sight (HMS or head aiming) and head-following flashlights. But when I wear Quest3, the HMS aiming is always a lot upwards. Most games cannot set the offset, and I have to lower my head to aim at the enemy in front, which hurts my eyes and neck.But this game has offset setting for the helmet-mounted sight! Just for this, I'll give it 5/5.
The severs I try to join are usually ghost towns when I look for a player hosted one

The greatest mech game I’ve played the physics and mechanics of the game really lets you immerse in the sheer size of the mech you are controlling so many weapons and mechs. Don’t know why this game is invisible on the Meta store should be highly Recommended.

I just bought the game and I can't even play it. The menu is bugged and while I can select things, I just see black void with ghost looking people around. I tried the tutorial and I fall through the non existent ground before crashing. Tried uninstalling and reinstalling but the same issues come up. Wish I could play it.

developer_response.body: This is an issue with the Meta store, which sometimes fails to send the quest user both app packages. In VOX's case, it requires 2 main files that Quest2/3 downloads, and when the 2nd file isn't downloaded, it will not funciton properly. Simply uninstalling it and reinstalling it, while ensuring you get a ~5gig download size will fix this issue with Meta's download system.

reviews.review_title: Worst game ive llayed on meta quest 2
reviews.review_description: This game must be designed for the quest 3 because on the quest 2 its boring, glitchy and slow as molasses. I couldnt even get through basic training because it wouldnt even register simple things like where I was looking, plus your weapons overheat WAY too fast. Boring bad game.
Not much else to say, terrible game.

reviews.review_title: Do not waste your time or money
reviews.review_description: Very unimpressed

I initially loved this game, until I got to the mission where you have to jump to the lightning to recharge your thrusters. I was fighting the controls far more than the enemies and kept failing the mission because I could not keep up. So frustrating, so I moved on to another game. I probably got my money's worth, however.

If you are more dextrous than me, or more patient, this could be a great game - you really feel like you are in a giant mech.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to review VOX, we appreciate it. Apart from the very first spire which is part of the tutorial, you aren't required to use the spires for the remainder of the mission. Furthermore this mission can be made much easier by selecting a lighter grinder such as Hopper, who can jumpjet much farther and refuels quicker. I'm sure that armed with this knowledge, you'll make short work of this mission yet! Also note that in the future, we'll be adding a dedicated jumpjetting tutorial option to empower players such as yourself to fully understand the finer nuances of the jumpjets and make more successful leaps and bounds.

reviews.review_description:
Best mech game you can get. but story is too slow,like you walking for 30 mins then fight for 5 mins. And they are talking too much while i just want to ride a robot and shoot some stuff.

And this game have zero comfortable option on cockpit ,so you will need strong vr immunity to play this game.

developer_response.body: Hi BigBoss. If the story isn't your cup of tea and you'd like to get to the action quicker, you can speed things up by skipping character dialog using the B button. You can also teleport around the ship quickly, and combining these 2 things will let you get to the missions in no time! For the cockpit experience, we do have a comfort setting we call "blinders" (find it in settings>video>blinders) which you can increase to the aggressive setting and that may help improve your comfort while turning the grinder left or right.

reviews.review_description: This game causes motion sickness for me…would like a refund but I guess I had it too long or something

I haven’t seen many people mention that it crashes every time you take off the oculus headset, or, press the ‘home’ button on controls. If you’re on the campaign this is quite frustrating as you have to quit and re-launch the game from the beginning of the mission every time. 

It is a fun game up until that point though.

developer_response.body: It's unfortunate the our game isn't working properly for you, it's certainly not intended to misbehave so frequently. We're unsure why this is the case for a rare few people, and have thus far been unable to reproduce this crashing behavior ourselves. Just to confirm, you are using a Quest2 headset? Also, do you make use of the cast feature? Finally, is your headset pretty filled with other apps or close to empty? Answers to questions like this may help us get closer to figuring out why VOX is crashing on your headset.

reviews.review_description:
Vox Machinae does a lot of things good, and is a great game for Mech VR game-seekers! The multiplayer is the real standout here, and the mech controls are very nicely designed, it really does feel like you're piloting a huge mech around the landscape as you fight off enemies and go through your missions! 

The real problem with the game is that the 'narrative' sections on the ship are incredibly tedious walk and click affairs during which the animations are wooden and wonky, the scripts are long and boring, and all it really does is get in the way of you continuing the single-player campaign. 

For our full review, search YouTube for '6DOF Reviews Vox Machinae'!

After trying to jump up on a rock 5 times unsuccessfully in the basic tutorial and given the flat textures and slow moving mechanics I gave up, definitely not the game for me. There are too many games well beyond this attempt in VR.

developer_response.body: thanks for giving it a shot. Our jumpjet mechanics definitely take some time getting used to. Oftentimes we find it's just a matter of getting a little more vertical lift (by lifting the stick up rather than just forwards or backwards) that helps players get to everywhere they need in the game with speed. Another thing to consider is picking one of the lighter grinders from the loadout customization menu, such as the hopper. Because of their lighter mass, they get much more lift can can help newer players get the hang of the physics in the game quicker.

reviews.review_description: Yes
Please help I paid for it but it won't install it's stick on install failed

developer_response.body: Hi there Finkitch, much like any app on the meta store, this can happen and isn't anything to do with our game. I recommend reaching out to meta, as they should be fixing this kind of stuff! However, as someone who's had this issue trying to install another game too, I can let you know what i did to fix it there. I went to settings>storage, and on the right side where there is a list of all the games on the device, I pressed the trashcan to remove the game that couldn't install. Then, I went back to the apps menu and tried to download again. That worked for me then, and I'm sure if you look online you'll find other suggestions if this didn't work. Keep at it, and I'm sure you'll get it going. Please consider revising your rating, with either your impressions, or removing this review since you were unable to play to share your impressions.

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game immediately because I thought: oh yeah! giant robot mech battles! And so, here's a similarly impulsive review. I might update this after some more time playing with it, but so far I'm happy.

PRO: oh yeah! giant mech battle!

PRO: The mech has a trucker horn that goes honk honk whenever you pull on it and I think that's awesome. 

PRO: You can control the mech realistically by manipulating the various doohickeys in the mech vehicle and/or you can map your controller to do the movements. neat!

PRO: Some of the voice acting is good.

CON: The UI for remapping the controls is cumbersome. Just give me a list of every function and then let me assign whatever I want to it. Don't break it up by which part of which controller, that's only going to slow things down.

CON: While some of the voice acting is pretty good, I can quickly tell that the voice of the mech's computer is going to get on my nerves. The sound of it is a little off to my ears, and also it's a bit repetitive telling you where to go while you're still on your way.

CON: You'll need to take some time to orient yourself to the controls. It's not un-learnable or anything but you don't get to dive right in to kicking butt like you might with a more intuitable FPS interface.

I think the story is a bit tongue-in-cheek. If you expect the cinematic narrative you might get from the Halo games for example, then you aren't really going to care much for the "drama" here, but if you're more inclined to take a "oh yeah, mech battle!" attitude like me then, well maybe you can just chuckle at the story parts and blast your way through. 

Overall, I expect this game to be fun and I don't regret the impulse buy.
I just wished that the Terrain Graphics were better ..how ever it s worth the money 💴 .. it gets a solid 3 ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ Stars from me

Other more eloquent users have written more comprehensive reviews, but my favorite part is there is a multi-player mode where you and your friends, or just yourself, can play against AI until you're comfortable enough for pvp combat.

reviews.review_title: I don't always right reviews but...
reviews.review_description: When I do it's for something incredible. Truth be told I have only played the tutorial and first mission, but that alone is enough for me to write this review. As a seasoned mechwarrior pilot having played every mechwarrior game released  I am amazed and anxious for this adventure. I want to thank the developer's for putting the time and effort into this project as I know from personal experience IT. WAS. NOT. EASY. and is very time consuming and trying work. For the average user the controls may be intimidating at first but for me I love it, it seems very challenging but rewarding at the same time. I will definitely be watching you with your next project, great job guy's it's a masterpiece.

I played a couple levels of the campaign and then I switched over to multiplayer just to check it out.  2 hours later, I realized I meant to stop playing an hour earlier.  I really got lost in this and felt like I was actually inside a giant mech.    Looking forward to playing again!
Have you ever felt too short? well how about you pilot a 100ft tall mech to get a taste of what its like to be taller than anybody. Is an awesome game

reviews.review_title: Bought it on the first day.
reviews.review_description: If you like Armored Core from back in the day, this is the vibe I get. It is dope, but takes some getting used to. Once you do, you may find yourself playing for hours before you know it.

reviews.review_title: The game I didn't know I wanted.
reviews.review_description: The play style and game play in this game are just so fun. The battles are epic and it feels natural to just sit and play this game.
If you were a fan of the old MechWarrior & Wing Commander games on PC back in the day you will absolutely love this game. Hands down the best game I've played on the standalone oculus. Get it!
Very immersive, great controls, lots of options. I highly recommend this game.
